tweets.text: RT @GOPMajorityWhip: It’s time to bring common sense back to banking, and the Main Street Capital Access Act does just that. This legislation will right-size federal bank regulations that currently treat local and mid-sized banks the same as the largest banks in the country. By cutting unnecessary red tape and expanding access to funding, we are going to keep community banks strong. We’re also pleased to see the Keeping Deposits Local Act included in this final bill. I want to thank Chairman @RepFrenchHill, @RepAndyBarr, and @FinancialCmte for their work on this legislation and helping keep community banks competitive.
tweets.summary: RT @GOPMajorityWhip: It’s time to bring common sense back to banking, and the Main Street Capital Access Act does just that. This legislation will…

tweets.text: RT @FinancialCmte: WATCH: Chairman @RepFrenchHill in support of H.R. 6955: "We're expanding access to capital, supporting American business, ensuring our financial system remains the most dynamic and diverse in the world. And this bill delivers on those goals. It spurs the formation of new banks. It brings back this common-sense tailoring to regulation. It restores fairness and transparency in bank supervision. It helps banks attract, retain, and diversify important funding sources from deposits. And it removes unnecessary barriers that have limited lending in communities across this country." 📺⬇️

tweets.text: 🚨NEW: @Sen_Alsobrooks tells me the White House’s idea to have the Department of Justice enforce the Clarity Act’s ethics provisions is an “unserious offer” and says she would not support the bill if that is the only enforcement option. Senator @berniemoreno, who has been working with @SenatorLummis to secure an ethics agreement with the White House, told reporters last night that the current proposal would put the DOJ, rather than state attorneys general, in charge of enforcement. “But we’ll keep working from that floor to reach an agreement that holds us all accountable,” Alsobrooks said. Alsobrooks is one of two Democratic senators who voted to advance the Clarity Act out of the Senate Banking Committee in May, and has said her continued support for the bill hinges on strong ethics provisions being included in the final text.
tweets.summary: 🚨NEW: @Sen_Alsobrooks tells me the White House’s idea to have the Department of Justice enforce the Clarity Act’s ethics provisions is an “unserious…
